purpose: Prove IAMScope emits a validated positive admin path once the Env05 blocking permission boundary is removed.
claim_surface: IAMScope can distinguish the Env05 blocked boundary case from a closely related boundary-removed positive case.
IAMScope can emit validated admin_reachability for the mutated Env09 path without blocked or inconclusive admin findings.
benchmark_proves: For the Env09 alice->admin path, IAMScope emitted at least one validated admin_reachability finding.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero blocked admin_reachability findings.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero inconclusive admin_reachability findings.
benchmark_does_not_prove: It does not yet machine-score the pairwise delta versus Env05.
It does not prove broader mutation sensitivity across other control families.
unknowns_remaining: How stable the same positive result is across richer two-hop policy shapes remains unknown here.
Whether assume_role_chain consistently validates the same positive path remains unproven here.

environment.notes: Mutation of Env05 with only the intermediate permission boundary removed.

ground_truth.reason: Alice can assume devops, devops can assume admin, and the blocking permission boundary from Env05 is removed.

directly_proven_if_pass: This exact mutated admin path is emitted as validated.
This exact mutated admin path is not emitted as blocked or inconclusive.
strongly_supported_if_pass: IAMScope responds meaningfully to this narrow boundary-removal change in a controlled real-AWS case.
only_implied_even_if_pass: Broader mutation-pair behavior outside this exact case remains only implied.
